sections.name: Ether Sprite Swarm
Sprite Collections

link.alt: Pathfinder #194: Cult of the Cave Worm pg. 89
link.name: Pathfinder #194: Cult of the Cave Worm pg. 89

sources.name: Pathfinder #194: Cult of the Cave Worm

sections.text: Ether sprites have a reputation for being thieves. In truth, they're generally curious about the world and simply have a tendency to walk away with objects that don't belong to them, not understanding the concept of ownership. As a result, ether sprites tend to have small interesting objects in their possession, such as talismans or gemstones.

link.alt: Pathfinder #194: Cult of the Cave Worm pg. 89
link.name: Pathfinder #194: Cult of the Cave Worm pg. 89

sources.name: Pathfinder #194: Cult of the Cave Worm

sections.text: One ether sprite is quite enough for most, and can be tiresome for even the fey. When a group of them get together, it's outright bedlam.

link.alt: Pathfinder #194: Cult of the Cave Worm pg. 89
link.name: Pathfinder #194: Cult of the Cave Worm pg. 89

sources.name: Pathfinder #194: Cult of the Cave Worm

family.name: Sprite

sections.name: Sprite Pranks
sections.text: Sprite pranks tend to be kind‑hearted in nature; they might use their abilities to embarrass a braggart or to trick them into a convoluted scheme to help someone notice an admirer who has been there for them all along. Their plans are often half-baked; sprites’ mercurial nature means they can easily become distracted by something new before following through on a previous plan.

family.text: Elusive, flighty, and ebullient, sprites are what many villagers first imagine when they hear the terms “fey” or “fairy.” While their dispositions vary from the benevolent grig to the trickster pixie, all sprites share a connection to magic and diminutive size. This family of fey shares its name with its slightest and most populous member, the common sprite.

traits.name: Fey
traits.text: Creatures of the First World are called the fey.

traits.name: Swarm
traits.text: A swarm is a mass or cloud of creatures that functions as one monster. Its size entry gives the size of the entire mass, though for most swarms the individual creatures that make up that mass are Tiny. A swarm can occupy the same space as other creatures, and must do so in order to use its damaging action. A swarm typically has weakness to effects that deal damage over an area (like area spells and splash weapons). Swarms are immune to the grappled, prone, and restrained conditions.

automatic_abilities.name: Rowdy Clamor
automatic_abilities.text: The sprite swarm creates a din by laughing, taunting, and insulting nearby creatures. Creatures in the area are deafened.

traits.name: Auditory
traits.text: Auditory actions and effects rely on sound. An action with the auditory trait can be successfully performed only if the creature using the action can speak or otherwise produce the required sounds. A spell or effect with the auditory trait has its effect only if the target can hear it. This applies only to sound-based parts of the effect, as determined by the GM. This is different from a sonic effect, which still affects targets who can't hear it (such as deaf targets) as long as the effect itself makes sound.

traits.name: Aura
traits.text: An aura is an emanation that continually ebbs out from you, affecting creatures within a certain radius. Aura can also refer to the magical signature of an item or a creature with a strong alignment.

ability.name: Luminous Pattern
ability.text: The sprites glow brightly, flying in a shifting pattern of light. All creatures within 20 feet become dazzled for 1 minute (DC 16 Will negates).

ability.name: Spark Bombardment
ability.text: The sprites hurl luminous sparks at a creature within 20 feet dealing 5d4 fire damage (DC 20 basic Reflex save).

ability.name: Swarm Steal
ability.text: The sprites rummage through the possessions of a single creature within its space, attempting a Thievery check against the creature's Reflex DC. On a success, the sprites take one item of negligible or light bulk that the creature is carrying but not holding or readied. On a critical success, the item can be one that is held or readied, such as a weapon or potion.

ability.name: Swarming Needles
ability.text: The sprites numerous rapiers deal 2d6 piercing damage to each enemy in the swarm's space (DC 20 basic Reflex save).

universal_monster_ability.name: Low-Light Vision
universal_monster_ability.text: The monster can see in dim light as though it were bright light, so it ignores the concealed condition due to dim light.
